Comment Support

Complete documentation for cell comments (legacy notes) in Aspose.Cells FOSS for .NET

← Back to Index

Comment Overview

The Comment class represents a worksheet comment (legacy note) anchored to a single cell with support for rich text content, author information, and visibility control.

Comment Features

  • Rich Text: Supports plain text with formatting
  • Author Information: Display and set author name
  • Visibility Control: Show or hide comments on hover or always visible
  • Custom Box Size: Set custom width and height in pixels
  • Multiple Comments: Add multiple comments per sheet
  • Cell Anchoring: Comments are tied to specific cells

Usage Examples

Adding Comments to Cells

using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;

var workbook = new Workbook();
var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

// Add comments to different cells
int comment1 = worksheet.Comments.Add("A1");
int comment2 = worksheet.Comments.Add("B1");
int comment3 = worksheet.Comments.Add("C1");
int comment4 = worksheet.Comments.Add("D1");

// Configure comments
worksheet.Comments["A1"].Author = "Reviewer 1";
worksheet.Comments["A1"].Note = "Please verify this calculation";
worksheet.Comments["A1"].IsVisible = true;

worksheet.Comments["B1"].Author = "Reviewer 2";
worksheet.Comments["B1"].Note = "Update needed by Friday";
worksheet.Comments["B1"].IsVisible = false;

// Hide comment by default
worksheet.Comments["C1"].IsVisible = false;

Console.WriteLine($"Added {worksheet.Comments.Count} comments");

Accessing Comment Properties

// Access a comment
var comment = worksheet.Comments["A1"];

Console.WriteLine($"Author: {comment.Author}");
Console.WriteLine($"Note: {comment.Note}");
Console.WriteLine($"Visible: {comment.IsVisible}");
Console.WriteLine($"Position: Row {comment.Row}, Column {comment.Column}");

// Modify comment
comment.Author = "Updated Author";
comment.Note = "This has been reviewed and approved";
comment.IsVisible = true;

Customizing Comment Appearance

using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;

var workbook = new Workbook();
var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

// Add comment with custom sizing
int commentIndex = worksheet.Comments.Add("B2");

var comment = worksheet.Comments[commentIndex];
comment.Author = "Designer";
comment.Note = "Please check the formatting";
comment.Width = 200;  // Width in pixels
comment.Height = 100; // Height in pixels
comment.IsVisible = true;

Console.WriteLine($"Created styled comment");

Comment Properties

The Comment class provides the following properties and methods:

  • Row - Zero-based row of annotated cell (read-only)
  • Column - Zero-based column of annotated cell (read-only)
  • Author - Comment author name
  • Note - Plain text content of the comment
  • IsVisible - Visibility state (show/hide)
  • Width - Comment box width in pixels (min: 1)
  • Height - Comment box height in pixels (min: 1)

CommentCollection

The CommentCollection class manages all comments in a worksheet:

  • Count - Number of comments
  • this[int] - Access comment by index
  • this[string] - Access comment by cell reference (e.g., "A1")
  • Add() - Add comment by coordinates or cell reference
  • RemoveAt(int) - Remove comment by index
  • RemoveAt(string) - Remove comment by cell reference
  • Parameters: Coordinates or cell name for Add; index for RemoveAt(int); cell name for RemoveAt(string)

Important Notes

  • Read-only Properties: Row and Column are read-only after the comment is added
  • Unique Comments: Each cell can only have one comment
  • Memory Management: Comments are stored efficiently in the worksheet model
  • Cell Reference: Comments use zero-based row and column coordinates
  • Rich Text: Comments support plain text but not HTML or markdown
